meta.name: Sub-2 Hour Half Marathon
meta.subtitle: Break the 2-hour barrier
meta.description: Sub-2 hours requires consistent 5:41/km pace. This 14-week plan builds the aerobic engine and race-specific fitness to hold that pace.

weeks.title: Base Building — Week 1
weeks.note: Week 1: Build your aerobic base. Every run should feel comfortable — you're conditioning your body to handle more.

sessions.det: 5km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.
5km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
11km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.title: Base Building — Week 2
weeks.note: Week 2: Build your aerobic base. Every run should feel comfortable — you're conditioning your body to handle more.

sessions.det: 6km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.
6km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
12km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.note: Week 3: Build your aerobic base. Every run should feel comfortable — you're conditioning your body to handle more.

sessions.det: 6km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.
6km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
13km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.title: Base Building — Week 4
weeks.note: Week 4: Build your aerobic base. Every run should feel comfortable — you're conditioning your body to handle more.

sessions.det: 5km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
4km very easy · 6:30–7:00/km · Shake out the legs. Slower than you think you need.
5km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
9km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.note: Week 5: Build your aerobic base. Every run should feel comfortable — you're conditioning your body to handle more.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.
7km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
14km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.title: Build Phase — Week 6
weeks.note: Week 6: Quality over quantity. The interval and tempo sessions are where fitness is made. Protect your sleep this week.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.
6km tempo · 4:45–5:05/km · Comfortably hard. You can speak in short sentences only.
13km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.title: Build Phase — Week 7
weeks.note: Week 7: Quality over quantity. The interval and tempo sessions are where fitness is made. Protect your sleep this week.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.
7km tempo · 4:45–5:05/km · Comfortably hard. You can speak in short sentences only.
14km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.title: Build Phase — Week 8
weeks.note: Week 8: Quality over quantity. The interval and tempo sessions are where fitness is made. Protect your sleep this week.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
4km very easy · 6:30–7:00/km · Shake out the legs. Slower than you think you need.
6km tempo · 4:45–5:05/km · Comfortably hard. You can speak in short sentences only.
11km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

8km tempo · 4:45–5:05/km · Comfortably hard. You can speak in short sentences only.
16km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.note: Week 10: Quality over quantity. The interval and tempo sessions are where fitness is made. Protect your sleep this week.

sessions.det: 9km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.

weeks.title: Build Phase — Week 11
weeks.note: Week 11: Quality over quantity. The interval and tempo sessions are where fitness is made. Protect your sleep this week.

sessions.det: 10km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.
8km tempo · 4:45–5:05/km · Comfortably hard. You can speak in short sentences only.
18km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.title: Peak Training — Week 12
weeks.note: Week 12: Peak training week. This is hard — it's meant to be. Trust the process and execute each session.

sessions.det: 7km easy then 4 × 100m strides · Start slow, build to 90% in the final 30m of each stride.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.
7km total · 400m reps at 5K race pace · Full recovery between reps. Quality over quantity.
17km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.title: Peak Training — Week 13
weeks.note: Week 13: Peak training week. This is hard — it's meant to be. Trust the process and execute each session.

sessions.det: 7km easy then 4 × 100m strides · Start slow, build to 90% in the final 30m of each stride.
45–60 min · Squats, lunges, hip thrusts, calf raises, core work · Injury prevention first.
7km total · 400m reps at 5K race pace · Full recovery between reps. Quality over quantity.
17km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.title: Taper — Week 14
weeks.note: Week 14: Trust the taper. The work is done. Freshen up the legs — no heroics.

sessions.det: 5km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
5km easy then 4 × 100m strides · Start slow, build to 90% in the final 30m of each stride.
9km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.
